items.briefing:
With the FIFA World Cup underway, Australians are being warned about a surge in scams targeting fans and workers. Research shows Australian football fans are connecting to fake Wi-Fi networks at venues, risking their personal data. Cybercriminals are also targeting World Cup staff and suppliers with phishing attacks. Separately, Microsoft has reported a serious security breach involving a zero-day vulnerability, though details remain limited.

A new training tool from KnowBe4 uses a game format to help staff recognise voice-based phishing ("vishing") scams, which are increasingly common in workplace settings.

**Tip:** Avoid using public or unknown Wi-Fi networks at events — use your mobile data instead.

featured.title: Microsoft, Australian Government sign MOU on cloud, cybersecurity and AI cooperation
featured.quote: Microsoft-Australian Government cybersecurity partnership signals national strategic focus on cloud and AI security.
